figures.caption: Fig. 11. SNR as a function of launch power per channel for SbL-DBP, EnDBP, and DBP under equal computational complexity constraints. Three representative complexity regimes are considered: low (≈ 3 · 102 RMpS, dotted), medium (≈ 103 RMpS, solid), and high (≈ 104 RMpS, dashed).

Fig. 6. SNR gain as a function of the number of steps Nst for SbL-DBP with different numbers of subbands Nsb ∈ {1, 2, 4, 8, 11}, the nonlinear memory is fixed to Nc = 8 (solid curves). Dashed curves correspond to filters optimized with symmetry and translational invariance constraints (sec. III-C).

Fig. 9. Improvement of the signal-to-noise ratio ∆SNR as a function of the computational complexity for DBP-based methods: DBP (green triangles), EnDBP (orange squares indicating all realizations for different Nc and Nst values), and SbL-DBP (blue circles indicating all realizations for different Nsb, Nc, and Nst values). The curves represent the Pareto-optimal sets. Red star markers represents SbL-DBP with sparsified MIMO coefficients.
Fig. 10. SNR gain as a function of the number of subbands Nsb for SbL-DBP under different computational complexity constraints.

Fig. 7. Normalized coefficients Cjlk for the case Nsb = 4, Nc = 16, Nst = 20 calculated without constraints (colored solid curves) and calculated with constraints on the conditions (15)-(17) (black dashed curves).
Fig. 1. Scheme of a proposed NLC consisting of analysis filter bank, a subband learned DBP module and synthesis filter bank with Nsb subbands.

Fig. 8. SNR gain ∆SNR as a function of the weight decay θ for the SbLDBP with Nst = 10, Nsb = 4, and Nc = 8. Inset: (a) distribution of the learned MIMO coefficient magnitudes for θ = 5 · 10−4, (b) coefficient distribution after sparsification.

Fig. 4. (a) End-to-end learning framework of the SbL-DBP, represented as a deep layered model with alternating linear and nonlinear blocks. The MIMO coefficients Cjlk are optimized jointly using gradient-based training. (b) Dynamics of training loss, validation loss, and learning rate, as functions of the training epoch.
